action_choice_hints: The candidate cell is adjacent to a region containing a distinct token role not present in the majority of the grid.
The candidate cell is part of a contiguous structure that connects two or more regions with different token roles.
The candidate cell is positioned such that selecting it would maintain the connectivity of a specific structure in the grid.
The candidate cell is not adjacent to any cell with a token role associated with instability or change.
The candidate cell is at the boundary between two regions with contrasting token roles.
feature_family_hints: Adjacency relations: Needed to determine if a candidate cell is next to cells with specific token roles.
Connectivity features: Required to assess whether selecting a cell preserves or alters the connectedness of structures.
Boundary detection: Useful for identifying cells that lie at the interface between regions of different token roles.
Contiguity features: To capture whether a cell is part of a continuous region with shared properties.
causal_transition_hints: Choosing a cell adjacent to a region with a distinct token role tends to trigger a change in the local configuration of that region.
Selecting a cell that maintains the connectivity of a structure tends to avoid fragmentation of that structure in the next state.
Choosing a cell at the boundary between regions tends to cause a transition where the boundary shifts or merges.
Selecting a cell not adjacent to unstable regions tends to avoid triggering transitions associated with instability.
